# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Twilio\Rest\Client;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Config;

class SmsService
{
    protected $client;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $accountSid = Config::get('services.twilio.sid');
        $authToken = Config::get('services.twilio.token');
        $this->client = new Client($accountSid, $authToken);
    }

    public function sendSms($to, $from, $message)
    {
        try {
            $this->client->messages->create(
                $to,
                [
                    'from' => $from,
                    'body' => $message
                ]
            );
            return ['status' => 'success', 'message' => 'SMS sent successfully'];
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            return ['status' => 'error', 'message' => $e->getMessage()];
        }
    }
}

// Configuration in config/services.php
return [
    'twilio' => [
        'sid' => env('TWILIO_SID'),
        'token' => env('TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN'),
        'from' => env('TWILIO_FROM'),
    ],
];

// .env file
// TWILIO_SID=your_twilio_sid
// TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=your_twilio_auth_token
// TWILIO_FROM=your_twilio_phone_number
